title: puccinellia walkeri subsp. chathamica
intro: Puccinellia walkeri subsp. chathamica, also known as Chatham Island saltbush, is a subspecies of the Puccinellia walkeri plant, a type of grass that is native to the Chatham Islands in New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chatham Island saltbush include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and optimal uses.
